source_metadata.title_raw: Marco Barcena - I-18-263 - December 19, 2018 - Bell Gardens

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
' 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Marco Barcena 
6036 Quinn St., Apt. A 
Bell Gardens, CA 90201 
December 19, 2018 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-18-263 
Dear Mr. Barcena: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Because your question does not involve a specific pending 
decision, we are treating this as a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329( c ). 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest, Government Code Section 53227, or restrictions on incompatible activities. Also note that 
we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any 
advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
QUESTIONS 
1. May you, as a newly-elected Bell Gardens City Councilmember, continue to hold 
your position as an employee of Bell Gardens' Recreation and Community Services 
Department? 
2. Will holding both positions create a conflict of interest under the Act? 
CONCLUSION 
1. Nothing in the Act prevents you from holding two positions simultaneously, either 
within a single agency or different agencies. However, we must caution that we offer 
no opinion on the application of Government Code Section 53227 or any restrictions 
on incompatible activities your agency may impose outside of the Act. You should 
consult your agency's counsel regarding these provisions as well as any other laws 
that may apply. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. I-18-263 
Page No. 2 
2. Under the Act, and based on the facts you have provided, you have no economic 
interest, and therefore no conflict of interest that arises from your two positions. 
However, as stated above, other laws outside of the Act may apply. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
You are currently employed by the City of Bell Gardens as a full-time Administrative 
Specialist for the Recreation and Community Services Department. You were recently elected as 
member of the City Council and will soon be sworn in. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or in any 
way attempting to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows 
or has reason to know he has a financial interest. As a Bell Gardens City Councilmember and an 
Administrative Specialist for the Recreation and Community Services Department, you are a public 
official in two capacities. (See Section 82048.) 
An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from 
its effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on, 
among other things: 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a).) 
• 
Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 
A public official's own personal finances, or those of a member of his or her immediate 
family. (Section 87103).) 
The only economic interest that your facts possibly implicate would be Section 87103(c), 
pertaining to sources of income. The Act, however, excludes from the definition of "income" any 
"salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem, and social security, disability or other similar 
benefit payments received from a state, local, or federal agency .... " (Section 82030(b)(2); see also 
Grayman Advice Letter, No. A-07-151; Grigg Advice Letter, No. 1-02-184.) Although you will 
presumably receive income from both of your positions, neither instance will constitute the type of 
income that establishes a disqualifying financial interest under the Act. (See Woodbury Advice 
Letter, No. 1-89-302; Darcy Advice Letter, No. I-87-296.) Accordingly, no conflict of interest arises 
under the Act merely by holding two governmental positions. 


File No. I-18-263 
Page No. 3 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
KMC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Kevin Cornwall 
Counsel, Legal Division
